model_answer: Collecting extra data can hurt privacy and trust. First limit collection, then explain retention because minimizing data lowers risk.
A likely bug is <= instead of < in the loop condition. Next, trace index values step by step.
Use .equals for String content checks because == compares references in Java objects.
Binary search repeatedly halves the sorted array, so it usually needs fewer comparisons than linear search.
Abstraction means hiding details and exposing a simpler interface for solving problems.
First inspect nested loop bounds, then profile where time is spent. Consider hash maps to reduce repeated scans.
